items.title: Mother's grief after toddler dies in apparent murder-suicide in Lismore
items.summary: A coronial inquest hears letters that arrived three days after an apparent murder-suicide in Lismore outlined a father's deadly plans.

items.title: Rebel Wilson defamation trial told Charlotte MacInnes was 'fudging' her version of producer incident
items.summary: Rebel Wilson's defamation trial hears from another producer on her directorial debut, who claims The Deb's lead actor was "fudging" her version of events about an alleged incident during production.

items.title: Retiring ACT chief magistrate Lorraine Walker on mistakes, rehabilitation and compassion
items.summary: Heart is not something anyone expects from the court system, but it was something outgoing ACT chief magistrate Lorraine Walker, who retired in March after 15 years in the job, aimed to portray.

items.title: Port Pirie locals worried about jobs as Nyrstar smelter's future in doubt
items.summary: Locals say the possible closure of the Port Pirie smelter and the loss of the 900 jobs associated with it would have ongoing effects on the city and the wider Spencer Gulf region.

items.title: Outback WA shire demands trucks get off road after beloved teacher killed in crash
items.summary: The death of a beloved school teacher in a crash with a road train has prompted a call to review the safety of an unsealed road in remote WA.

items.title: Wollongong tradesman jailed for robbing paedophile in Lightning Ridge heist
items.summary: A Wollongong man drove to Lightning Ridge with his police officer friend to rob an elderly convicted paedophile of $100,000.

items.title: Matt Glaezter, cancer survivor, world champion and two-time Olympic medallist, confirms retirement
items.summary: Matt Glaetzer was, for so long, the nearly man of cycling, heartbreakingly missing out on an elusive Olympic medal despite reaching five finals. But the South Australian refused to be beaten, either by that, or by cancer.

items.title: Putin condemns Ukrainian drone strikes against oil refineries deep inside Russian territory
items.summary: Ukraine says its drones have struck an oil refinery deep inside Russia, marking the latest in a series of attacks on oil facilities behind the front lines as Kyiv seeks to curb Moscow’s energy revenues used to fund the war.

items.title: Queensland Rail commuter services to switch to Saturday timetable amid pay dispute
items.summary: Queensland commuters can expect more delays from Tuesday when a Saturday service will start due to industrial action.

items.title: US Congress approves bill to end record Homeland Security funding shutdown
items.summary: "It is about damn time," said one Democrat as US politicians voted to end the funding freeze for most of the department's agencies after Mr Trump's deadly immigration operations in Minneapolis.

items.title: Reza Adib, partner of former Queensland premier Annastacia Palaszczuk, charged with rape
items.summary: Vahid Reza Adib, the partner of former Queensland premier Annastacia Palaszczuk, has been charged with three counts of rape relating to alleged offending against a woman in her 30s at Burleigh Heads on March 30.

items.title: At a Melbourne hospital, nitrous oxide users are presenting with psychosis
items.summary: Harms from nitrous oxide have been growing in Australia in recent years, with the substance linked to a growing number of accidental deaths, while a study from a Melbourne hospital shows abuse of the inhalant is associated with psychosis.

items.title: Mount Isa City Council moves to sell homes over unpaid rates in outback mining city
items.summary: In Mount Isa, nearly 50 properties, some abandoned, have accrued years of unpaid rates and could be forced to sale.

items.title: The home battery rebate is changing. Is it still worth it and what's the right size?
items.summary: In less than nine months, more than a quarter of a million batteries were installed in Australian households. Here's how the battery rebate is changing and why.

items.title: Alice Springs erupts in violence after 5yo girl's alleged killer arrested
items.summary: Wild scenes erupted on the streets of Alice Springs last night following the arrest of Jefferson Lewis over the alleged murder of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby.

items.title: Victorian bottle shops awash with illicit alcohol, researchers find
items.summary: The National Drug Research Institute found 30 per cent of bottle shops it audited across different areas of Melbourne and regional Victoria were stocking illicit alcohol.

items.title: SA premier compares course upgrade for in-doubt LIV Golf with similar AFL Gather Round investments
items.summary: Peter Malinauskas compares spending $45 million to redevelop the North Adelaide golf course to similar funding for the AFL to play in the Barossa Valley, as media reports suggest Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund is preparing to pull financial support for LIV Golf.

items.title: Chalmers does little to stop capital gains tax debate as budget looms
items.summary: Seven years ago, proposed changes to capital gains tax and negative gearing were named as part of the reason Labor lost the unlosable election. Times have changed.

items.title: Global Sumud Flotilla carrying Australians intercepted on its way to Gaza by IDF
items.summary: The Global Sumud Flotilla has been intercepted by the IDF, an official tweet by the group says.

items.title: Queensland man James Lake-Kusviandy Blee on trial over Newcastle cocaine haul
items.summary: Queensland man James Lake-Kusviandy Blee is on trial over a cocaine shipment into Newcastle.

items.title: Melbourne City to face 'team we never want to lose to' in A-League Women semifinal
items.summary: Taylor Otto sees a lot of potential in the Australian domestic league. She thinks it just needs a bigger investment to get there.

items.title: Narrandera residents fed up with murky tap water
items.summary: When residents of Narrandera, in southern NSW, turn on the tap, the water that comes out is sometimes so dirty it stains their clothes.

items.title: Two men denied bail after allegedly flying meth in drone in Wollongong
items.summary: Police allege a drone was used to deliver meth in Wollongong before it crashed, leading to a wider investigation.

items.title: NT diphtheria outbreak worsens as cases of the potentially deadly disease more than double
items.summary: Cases of a deadly, vaccine-preventable strain of diphtheria have now risen to 18, after the Centre for Disease Control first declared an outbreak last month.

items.title: Gambling among Australian construction workers well above national average, creating safety risks
items.summary: New research reveals 89 per cent of construction workers have gambled in the past year — higher than the national average.

items.title: Lebanon ceasefire not working because free rein given to Israel, says deputy prime minister Tarek Mitri
items.summary: Lebanese Deputy Prime Minister Tarek Mitri says the government cannot exercise its authority over the country while Israel continues its bombardments and occupation.

items.title: Coalition's risky Farrer strategy could clear path for One Nation
items.summary: Pauline Hanson is now set to tour more regional Coalition-held seats in her "sexy" new private plane, and the Liberal and National parties are helping clear the runway.

items.title: See how the price of everything has changed with inflation
items.summary: If it feels like everything is getting more expensive, that's because it is. With the rate of inflation rising this week, we've tracked how the price of everyday items has increased.

items.title: King Charles III visits September 11 memorial as he tours New York
items.summary: King Charles III and Queen Camilla met survivors and family members of victims, as well as with New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani.

items.title: Pauline Hanson billed taxpayers for flights to fundraisers and travel to catch Rinehart jet to Florida
items.summary: One Nation leader Pauline Hanson billed taxpayers for two trips that included party fundraisers, discussions with disaffected National Barnaby Joyce, a Gina Rinehart event and return flights to connect the senator to her private jet to Florida, sponsored by her major donor, Mrs Rinehart.

items.title: China inching towards resuming fuel exports as Penny Wong presses Australia's case
items.summary: Chinese state-owned oil companies have agreed to negotiate directly with Australian companies on jet fuel after the foreign minister's visit to China.

items.title: Fremantle Dockers player Nathan O'Driscoll opens up about mental health struggles in emotional post
items.summary: Fremantle Dockers player Nathan O'Driscoll reveals his mental health struggles in an emotional social media post, amid a push from players for the AFL to implement a mental health round.

items.title: Perth women share experiences of infection and surgery after nail salon visits
items.summary: More Perth women share their experience of needing surgery after having an adverse reaction to nail salon services, triggering a warning from the consumer watchdog.

items.title: Inflation details may give RBA pause for thought on interest rates
items.summary: While inflation remains well above the Reserve Bank's target, there are details in the numbers that may give it reasons to hold off another rate hike in May.

items.title: Hundreds of jobs to go at Home Affairs department in voluntary redundancy drive
items.summary: The secretary of the second-largest department says she has been trying to reduce headcount by natural attrition, but that further job cuts are needed by the end of the financial year to fit within the department's budget.

items.title: Fuel security a 'tricky balancing act', experts say as budget nears
items.summary: As the international fuel crisis continues to cause concern, the federal government is under pressure to provide a fix during next month's budget.

items.title: Big tech hits back at Labor government's Media Bargaining Incentive plan
items.summary: US tech giant Meta has condemned Labor's plan to tax large digital platforms that fail to pay for using Australian journalism as "government-mandated transfer of wealth".

items.title: Coalition points to next-generation stealth bombers as potential AUKUS stopgap
items.summary: The Coalition says Australia should consider buying next-generation stealth bombers to help bridge the gap until the AUKUS submarines arrive.

items.title: Penny Wong will have to navigate China, Japan and South Korea tensions
items.summary: When Foreign Minister Penny Wong travels to Japan, China and South Korea this week, she will move through a region where relationships point in different directions at the same time.

items.title: Train collision near Jakarta kills 14 as rescuers complete evacuation
items.summary: The train operator says the crash, on the outskirts of Jakarta, involved a commuter train and a long-distance train.

items.title: Australia's minimum fuel reserves would double under $800m Coalition plan, at a cost to drivers
items.summary: Australia would have 60 days' worth of fuel supplies in reserve under an $800 million Coalition proposal that would cost drivers one cent per litre.

items.title: Prediction markets accused of insider trading and profiting from state secrets
items.summary: A US soldier accused of using classified information to place bets on a covert military operation has thrown a spotlight on the fast-growing world of prediction markets.

items.title: Japan's north struck by magnitude-6.1 earthquake, followed by wildfires prompting evacuations
items.summary: A preliminary magnitude-6.1 earthquake struck northern Japan early today, the US Geological Survey has reported, but there are no immediate reports of fatalities or damage.

items.title: Bad Company: Art and big business collide in new Anne Edmonds, Kitty Flanagan comedy
items.summary: Everything Anne Edmonds has done over the last two decades has been leading to Bad Company, an unfettered satire of the theatre world the comedian created, wrote and stars in alongside Kitty Flanagan.

items.title: China's 'quirky' fix for property crisis rewards marathon runners, AI experts and tennis players
items.summary: Local governments in China are offering marathon runners, AI experts and tennis players subsidies to purchase unwanted properties amid a lingering real estate downturn.

items.title: Trump is political kryptonite in Australia but diplomacy still guides the way
items.summary: As Donald Trump's approval in Australia sinks, Anthony Albanese needs to walk a careful line: keep voters convinced of the merits of the alliance, while avoiding the impression he is being too reverential.

items.title: Sex workers speak out about the HIV crisis in Papua New Guinea
items.summary: Workers have told the ABC about the battle to stay safe as the Pacific nation battles a rising number of infections.

items.title: Why Anzac Day Test between Australia and New Zealand means so much to Wallaroos star Piper Duck
items.summary: Any time Piper Duck pulls on the Australian jersey is a special moment, but playing in the historic Anzac Day Test holds even greater significance for the Wallaroos back-rower.

items.title: Dozen more Aboriginal burial sites detected amid construction on Rottnest Island
items.summary: Authorities believe there may be a dozen more unmarked Aboriginal burial sites near where human remains were found on Western Australia's tourist haven Rottnest Island earlier this month.

items.title: Italy dismisses Trump official's 'shameful' idea to replace Iran at football World Cup
items.summary: Italian officials say they are not interested in replacing Iran at the FIFA World Cup, after the idea was raised by a Trump ally.

items.title: When is the Anzac Day public holiday? Which states have Monday off?
items.summary: Anzac Day is observed on April 25 every year. This year that date falls on a Saturday, but that doesn't mean a long weekend for everyone.

items.title: Mouse plague fears deepen as chemical regulator rejects CSIRO bait research
items.summary: Farmers are pushing for stronger bait approval as mice numbers soar out of control in Western Australia, but stronger poisons are being rejected by the national chemical regulator.

items.title: Waste-to-energy plant proposed for Vuda Point in Fiji sparks fierce backlash
items.summary: The controversial project would see waste burned to create energy in Fiji to reduce the country's reliance on fossil fuels, but locals and environmentalists are not happy.

items.title: Pacific leaders declare emergency over potential fuel shortages
items.summary: The prime minister of Solomon Islands says Pacific leaders have declared an emergency over the looming threat of fuel shortages, while the Australian government contemplates a plan which could see stocks of diesel held by the US military sent to Pacific nations suffering shortages.

items.title: By rejecting his bid for appeal the High Court has made Bruce Lehrmann's defamation case a complete failure
items.summary: The High Court was Bruce Lehrmann's last legal avenue to challenge a Federal Court ruling that, on the balance of probabilities, he raped Brittany Higgins — now that avenue has closed.

items.title: Combatting concussion in contact sport
items.summary: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

items.title: Warren Purse says paramedics in the NT are under pressure
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: First major suspects in Irma Palasics murder investigation were family members, court hears
items.summary: The lead investigator in the trial of two Melbourne men over the 1999 murder of Canberra grandmother Irma Palasics tells a court his first major suspects were Mrs Palasics's family members.

items.title: Astrid Linder designed the world's first average female crash test dummy
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Fire ant authority considers penalties for business that spread nests to central Qld
items.summary: The National Fire Ant Eradication Program is considering "enforcement actions" for a business accused of spreading nests to Queensland mine sites via a shipment of bricks.

items.title: Sir Chris Hoy's terminal cancer diagnosis greeted with outpouring of support
items.summary: Celebrities, athletes and politicians have rallied behind one of Britain's greatest Olympians, Sir Chris Hoy, who revealed he had just two to four years to live following a terminal cancer diagnosis.
